stories.headline: Bitcoin Stalls Below $80K as Pentagon-Backed Inflation Warning Flips Risk Mood
stories.tldr: BTC's push toward $80,000 stalled at $77.8K as rising oil and Strait of Hormuz risk reignited inflation fears across risk assets.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: a perp-driven rally without spot bid is fragile, meaning any macro shock can unwind weeks of gains in hours.
the_details: BTC traded $77,809 at 9 a.m. ET, down $385 on the day after failing $80K twice this week.
U.S. spot BTC ETFs logged their strongest weekly inflows in a month, but funding signals dominate spot.
ETH dropped 2.5%, SOL fell 2.49% to $85.74 as DFX index lost 2.7% since UTC midnight.

stories.headline: Kelp Hacker Launders 75,700 ETH Into Bitcoin via THORChain in 36 Hours
stories.tldr: The KelpDAO exploiter swapped 75,700 ETH into BTC through THORChain in roughly 36 hours, pushing the bridge's volume to $800 million.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: THORChain just absorbed a nine-figure laundering flow with zero throttling, reigniting the debate over neutral bridges and OFAC exposure.
the_details: The 36-hour swap generated about $910,000 in fees for THORChain LPs and node operators.
Original April 19 exploit drained 116,500 rsETH (~$292M), 18% of token supply, the largest 2026 hack.
TRM and Elliptic have flagged Lazarus Group as the likely actor behind both the Kelp and Drift breaches.

stories.headline: GSR Goes Live With BESO ETF Bundling Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana Exposure
stories.tldr: Market maker GSR launched its first ETF, BESO, an actively managed fund spanning Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana exposure.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: a sell-side desk crossing into ETF issuance signals further blurring of CeFi and TradFi distribution rails for crypto.
the_details: BESO is GSR's first U.S.-listed product after a decade as a top crypto liquidity provider.
Active management lets GSR rebalance BTC/ETH/SOL weights instead of tracking a fixed index.
Launch lands in a week SOL is the worst top-10 performer, down 2.5% to $85.74.

stories.headline: Mastercard Joins Blockchain Security Standards Council as Founding Member
stories.tldr: Mastercard signed on as a founding member of the Blockchain Security Standards Council to help shape security and privacy frameworks for tokenized assets.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: the world's #2 card network is now writing the rulebook on tokenization security, accelerating institutional comfort with on-chain settlement.
the_details: Council will draft frameworks covering blockchain networks, tokenized assets and wallet custody.
Mastercard adds payments-rail expertise to a body previously dominated by crypto-native firms.
Move follows April's wave of infra hacks topping $606M, the worst month since Feb 2025.

stories.headline: Blockchain Capital Hunting $700M Across Two New Crypto Funds, Bloomberg Says
stories.tldr: Crypto VC Blockchain Capital is raising $700 million across two new funds, per Bloomberg, signaling LP appetite is returning despite April's hack carnage.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: a $700M check-writing pipeline reopens early-stage funding for protocols starved since the late-2024 altcoin bear started.
the_details: Filing reported by Bloomberg early Thursday, April 23, 2026, around 4:37 a.m. EDT.
Two-fund structure mirrors industry trend of separate liquid token and equity vehicles.
Raise lands amid Pantera's view that altcoins have been in a bear market since late 2024.
